_provenance: FROZEN ORACLE — DO NOT EDIT. Hand-authored by the human conductor and committed BEFORE afk dispatch, so the executor asserts against a frozen file it cannot write (the same trust model as the brain_map-generated simple/expected.json). brain_map.py is hardcoded to my-brain and cannot generate an oracle for a foreign vault, so this second fixture is verified by hand: it uses different folder names (docs/refs/misc/daily) and a distinct topology (2 clusters of sizes 4 and 3, an articulation point, a transient daily note) to prove graphmark generalizes and is not hardcoded. pagerank was frozen for afk #4 via networkx's pure-python reference (_pagerank_python, alpha=0.85), cross-checked to reproduce simple/expected.json's pagerank exactly — so a correct pure-python impl must match BOTH fixtures, closing the single-fixture overfitting gap. If you believe a value is wrong, flag it — do not edit it to match your code.
